books.title: Or What You Will
books.author: Jo Walton
books.description: He has been a dragon, a scholar, a warrior, a god—but now he's merely a spark of idea in the mind of Sylvia Harrison, 73, award-winning author. When she starts a new fantasy novel, he must convince her they can step off the wheel of mortality.

books.title: The Thursday Murder Club
books.author: Richard Osman
books.description: In a peaceful retirement village, four unlikely friends meet once a week to investigate unsolved murders. When a brutal killing takes place on their very doorstep, Elizabeth, Joyce, Ibrahim and Ron—all pushing eighty—find themselves in the middle of their first live case.
books.awards: Barry Award, Anthony Award, ITW Thriller Award, Edgar Award

books.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
books.author: Holly Jackson
books.description: Everyone knows Sal Singh murdered Andie Bell five years ago. But Pippa Fitz-Amobi isn't so sure. When she chooses the case for her final project, she starts uncovering secrets someone desperately wants hidden—and if the real killer is still out there, how far will they go to stop her?
books.awards: Goodreads Choice Award
books.series: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder

books.title: Ducks, Newburyport
books.description: An Ohio housewife latticing cherry pies tries to bridge the gaps between reality and the torrent of meaningless information that is the United States of America—a scorching indictment of America's barbarity.

books.title: My Lovely Wife
books.author: Samantha Downing
books.description: We look like a normal couple. We're your neighbors, the parents of your kid's friend. We all have secrets to keeping a marriage alive. Ours just happens to be getting away with murder. A darkly comic psychological thriller about a couple whose boredom leads to something unthinkable.
books.awards: Edgar Award, Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: The Hands of the Emperor
books.author: Victoria Goddard
books.description: Cliopher Mdang is the personal secretary of the Last Emperor of Astandalas. He has spent more time with the Sun-on-Earth than any other person. One day, he invites the Emperor home to the Vangavaye-ve for a holiday. The acceptance upends the world.

books.title: The Silent Patient
books.author: Alex Michaelides
books.description: Alicia Berenson shoots her husband five times in the face—and then never speaks another word. Therapist Theo Faber becomes obsessed with uncovering her motive, taking him down a twisting path that threatens to consume him in this shocking psychological thriller.
books.awards: Barry Award, Goodreads Choice Award, Dublin Literary Award

books.title: Wanderers
books.description: Shana wakes up to find her sister in the grip of a strange malady—sleepwalking with inexorable determination toward an unknown destination, joining a flock of Americans on the same mysterious journey. This is the story of those who follow to protect them.

books.title: The Last Time I Lied
books.author: Riley Sager
books.description: Two summers at Camp Nightingale, fifteen years apart. First as a camper when three friends vanish without a trace; then invited back as a counselor, where the disappearances begin again. Emma must uncover what happened before another girl goes missing—and before the truth about her own role is revealed.

books.title: The Romance of the Three Kingdoms
books.author: Luo Guanzhong

books.title: The Widows of Malabar Hill
books.author: Sujata Massey
books.description: Bombay, 1921: Perveen Mistry is one of India's first female lawyers. When she investigates the will of a deceased mill owner, she finds three widows in purdah who can't speak to a male lawyer—and begins to suspect that one of them might be in danger.
books.awards: Agatha Award, Anthony Award, Macavity Award, Mary Higgins Clark Award

books.author: Paul Auster
books.description: Archibald Ferguson is born on March 3, 1947. From that single beginning, his life takes four simultaneous and independent fictional paths—four identical Fergusons made of the same DNA who go on to lead four entirely different lives.

books.title: If We Were Villains
books.author: M.L. Rio
books.description: Oliver Marks served ten years for a murder he may or may not have committed. As one of seven young actors studying Shakespeare at an elite arts college, they each played the same roles onstage and off. When the casting changes and one of them turns up dead, the rest face their greatest acting challenge yet.

books.title: Magpie Murders
books.author: Anthony Horowitz
books.description: Editor Susan Ryeland receives crime writer Alan Conway's latest manuscript—Atticus Pünd investigates a murder at an English manor. But the more Susan reads, the more she realizes there's another story hidden in the pages—one of ambition, jealousy, and real-world murder.
books.awards: Barry Award, Anthony Award, RUSA CODES Reading List

books.title: The Odyssey
books.author: Homer
books.description: In this first English translation by a woman, The Odyssey comes alive in vivid, contemporary idiom. A poem about violence and the aftermath of war, wealth and power, marriage and family, and the yearning for home.

books.title: The Seven Husbands of Evelyn Hugo
books.author: Taylor Jenkins Reid

books.title: A Great Reckoning
books.author: Louise Penny
books.description: Rachel takes the same commuter train every morning and evening. Every day she rattles down the track, staring at a stretch of houses, and stops at the signal that allows her to watch the perfect couple. Then she sees something shocking. When the woman she observed goes missing, Rachel finds herself deep in the investigation.
books.awards: Ned Kelly Award, Goodreads Choice Award, Book of the Month Book of the Year Award

books.title: The Couple Next Door
books.author: Shari Lapena
books.description: Jack and Grace seem like the ideal couple—so devoted to one another that Jack refuses to travel without Grace. Pity. Grace's friends wish they had what she has. But they don't know what goes on behind closed doors. A twisty domestic thriller about a marriage with a terrible secret.

books.title: The Historian
books.author: Elizabeth Kostova
books.description: Late one night, a young woman finds an ancient book and a cache of yellowing letters in her father's library. The letters are addressed to 'My dear and only love' and will draw her into a centuries-spanning mystery.

books.title: A Little Life
books.author: Hanya Yanagihara
books.description: Four college friends move to New York: actor Willem, painter JB, architect Malcolm, and enigmatic Jude, whose mind and body are scarred by an unspeakable childhood. Over decades, their relationships deepen and darken.

books.title: The Girl on the Train
books.author: Paula Hawkins
books.description: Forty years ago, Harriet Vanger disappeared from her family's island gathering. Her uncle is convinced she was murdered—by a member of his own family. He hires disgraced journalist Mikael Blomkvist and hacker Lisbeth Salander to uncover the truth in this sweeping Nordic crime novel.
books.awards: CWA Gold Dagger, Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: A Brief History of Seven Killings
books.author: Marlon James

books.title: All the Light We Cannot See
books.description: Marie-Laure lives near the Museum of Natural History in Paris. When the Nazis occupy Paris, she and her father flee to Saint-Malo. A German orphan boy, Werner, becomes an expert at building radios and is enlisted to track the resistance.

books.title: Gone Girl
books.author: Gillian Flynn
books.description: Reporter Camille Preaker returns to her small Missouri hometown to cover a series of murders—the second girl found with her teeth pulled out. But the town and her troubled family keep getting in the way of the story. Camille is fighting demons of her own—scars she hides under layers of clothing.
books.awards: Ian Fleming Steel Dagger Award, Ned Kelly Award

books.title: The Goldfinch
books.author: Donna Tartt
books.description: Aged thirteen, Theo Decker survives an accident that tears his life apart. Alone in New York, he clings to the thing that most reminds him of his mother: a small painting called The Goldfinch. It draws him into the criminal underworld.

books.title: A Fine Balance
books.author: Rohinton Mistry
books.description: The year is 1975 in an unnamed Indian city. The government has just declared a State of Emergency. Four strangers—a spirited widow, a young student, and two tailors who have fled caste violence—will be thrust together, forced to share one cramped apartment.

books.title: I Hotel
books.author: Karen Tei Yamashita
books.description: A multi-voiced epic of America's civil rights struggle in San Francisco's Chinatown. Beginning in 1968, students, laborers, artists, and revolutionaries make their way through history—united by the fight to save the International Hotel.

books.title: Midnight's Children
books.author: Salman Rushdie
books.description: Saleem Sinai is born at the stroke of midnight on August 15, 1947—the very moment of India's independence. His every act is mirrored in national events; he is linked by telepathy with India's 1,000 other 'midnight's children.'

books.title: Wolf Hall
books.author: Hilary Mantel
books.description: England in the 1520s: if the king dies without a male heir, civil war looms. Henry VIII wants to annul his marriage and marry Anne Boleyn. Into this impasse steps Thomas Cromwell—a charmer and a bully, astute in reading people, implacable in his ambition.

books.author: Roberto Bolaño
books.description: Academics, convicts, an American sportswriter, an elusive German novelist, and a teenage student caring for her widowed father—their lives intersect in the desert sprawl of Santa Teresa, where hundreds of young factory workers have disappeared.

books.title: The Girl With the Dragon Tattoo
books.author: Stieg Larsson
books.description: Precious Ramotswe sets up the only detective agency in Botswana, solving mysteries that are less murder and more human—missing husbands, wandering parents, fake witch doctors. A warm, gently comic celebration of Africa and its people.

books.title: Murder on the Orient Express
books.author: Agatha Christie
books.description: Three women, one dead husband, and one very complicated kindergarten. Jane moves to a wealthy beachfront town where Madeleine and Celeste befriend her. What starts with a schoolyard slight escalates into something dark—and eventually deadly.
books.awards: CWA Gold Dagger, Davitt Award, Ned Kelly Award, Australian Book Industry Award (ABIA), Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: The Pillars of the Earth
books.author: Ken Follett
books.description: A twelfth-century monk is driven to build the greatest Gothic cathedral the world has ever known. Set in feudal England, filled with intrigue, romance, and the ravages of war—one of the most beloved historical novels ever written.

books.title: The Terror
books.author: Dan Simmons
books.description: The 1845 Franklin Expedition has every expectation of triumph. But stranded in the Arctic, 126 men face poisonous food, dwindling coal, ships buckling in crushing ice—and something out there in the frigid darkness, a monstrous terror clawing to get in.

books.title: War and Peace
books.author: Leo Tolstoy
books.description: War and Peace centers on Napoleon's invasion of Russia in 1812 and follows three characters: Pierre Bezukhov, fighting for his inheritance; Prince Andrei, leaving his family for war; and Natasha Rostov, who intrigues both men.

books.title: Little, Big
books.author: John Crowley
books.description: The epic story of Smoky Barnable, who travels by foot to a place called Edgewood to marry Daily Alice Drinkwater, as was prophesied. Four generations of a singular family, living in a house on the magical border of an otherworld.

books.title: Murder at the Vicarage
books.author: Agatha Christie
books.description: 'Anyone who murdered Colonel Protheroe would be doing the world a favour,' declared the parson—a careless remark that came back to haunt him when the Colonel was found shot dead in his study. As Miss Marple soon discovers, the whole village had a motive.

books.title: The A.B.C. Murders
books.author: Agatha Christie
books.description: A serial killer leaves the ABC Railway Guide beside each victim's body. A is for Alice Asher in Andover; B is for Betty Bernard in Bexhill. As Hercule Poirot races to stay one step ahead, he must unravel why this is happening before the next letter arrives.

books.title: The Murder of Roger Ackroyd
books.author: Agatha Christie
books.description: Ten strangers are lured to an isolated island mansion and murdered one by one—each death mirroring a verse of a children's rhyme. As the tension builds and the guests turn on each other, the question isn't who did it but who could possibly survive. The world's best-selling mystery novel.
books.awards: Agatha Award

books.title: A Study in Scarlet
books.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
books.description: The unnamed narrator marries the dashing Maxim de Winter and moves into his imposing estate, Manderley. But the shadow of his beautiful first wife Rebecca hangs over everything—and the sinister housekeeper Mrs. Danvers ensures she is never forgotten.

books.title: A Suitable Boy
books.author: Vikram Seth
books.description: Lata and her mother Mrs. Rupa Mehra are both trying to find a suitable boy for Lata to marry. Set in India in the early 1950s, a sweeping panoramic portrait of four extended families and their lives, loves, and ambitions.

books.title: And Then There Were None
books.author: Agatha Christie
books.description: On the morning of their fifth wedding anniversary, Nick Dunne's wife Amy disappears. The police immediately suspect Nick. But as clues emerge, the marriage begins to unravel—and neither Nick's story nor Amy's diary can be trusted. A razor-sharp thriller about the lies we tell each other and ourselves.
books.awards: CWA Gold Dagger, Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: Cloud Atlas
books.author: David Mitchell
books.description: Cloud Atlas begins in 1850 with an American notary voyaging from the Chatham Isles, then jumps through time to 1931 Belgium, 1970s California, present-day England, a Korean superstate, and post-apocalyptic Hawaii—then boomerangs back through the same route in reverse.

books.title: David Copperfield
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.description: David Copperfield is the story of a young man's adventures from an unhappy impoverished childhood to the discovery of his vocation as a novelist. Among the vivid cast: his tyrannical stepfather, the eternally humble Uriah Heep, and enchanting Dora Spenlow.

books.title: Jonathan Strange & Mr Norrell
books.author: Susanna Clarke
books.description: By the early 1800s, English magicians have long lost the ability to perform magic. Then Mr Norrell of Yorkshire regains some of the powers of England's magical past. A rival magician appears: Jonathan Strange. Their ideas about English magic are very different.

books.title: The Secret History
books.author: Donna Tartt
books.description: Under their charismatic classics professor, a group of clever misfits at an elite New England college discover a way of living that is a world away from their contemporaries. When they go beyond normal morality, they slip gradually into corruption and evil.

books.title: North and South
books.author: Elizabeth Gaskell
books.description: When her father leaves the Church, Margaret Hale is uprooted to the industrial town of Milton. She develops a passionate sense of social justice—and a tempestuous relationship with mill-owner John Thornton, whose treatment of employees she opposes.

books.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
books.author: Alexandre Dumas
books.description: Thrown in prison for a crime he has not committed, Edmond Dantes learns of a great treasure and becomes determined not only to escape but to destroy the three men responsible for his imprisonment.
books.series: Le Comte de Monte-Cristo / The Count of Monte Cristo

books.title: Perdido Street Station
books.author: China Miéville
books.description: New Crobuzon is a squalid city where humans, Re-mades, and arcane races live in perpetual fear. Isaac, a brilliant scientist, is asked to restore a bird-man's power of flight. One lab specimen threatens the whole city—a vividly colored caterpillar consuming a hallucinatory drug.

books.title: The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay
books.author: Michael Chabon

books.title: The Blind Assassin
books.author: Margaret Atwood
books.description: Iris opens with: 'Ten days after the war ended, my sister drove a car off the bridge.' Margaret Atwood's novel-within-a-novel follows two sisters whose entangled lives of deception and love unfold across decades and novels.

books.title: The Hound of the Baskervilles
books.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
books.description: In 1935, thirteen-year-old Briony Tallis witnesses events she doesn't fully understand, then makes an accusation that shatters three lives. Decades later, she grapples with her guilt in one of the twentieth century's great novels about war, love, and the unreliability of memory.
books.awards: National Book Critics Circle Award, Whitbread Novel Award, IMPAC Dublin Literary Award

books.title: Villette
books.author: Charlotte Brontë
books.description: Lucy Snowe flees England and a tragic past to become an instructor in a French boarding school in Villette. There she unexpectedly confronts feelings of love and longing—and the heartache she has tried so long to escape. Brontë's most accomplished work.

books.title: House of Leaves
books.author: Mark Z. Danielewski
books.description: A young family moves into a small home on Ash Tree Lane where they discover their house is bigger on the inside than on the outside. The consequences of that impossibility prove impossible to face.

books.title: Cryptonomicon
books.author: Neal Stephenson
books.description: In 1942, mathematical genius Lawrence Waterhouse is assigned to keep the Nazis ignorant that the Allies cracked the Enigma code. In the present, his crypto-hacker grandson attempts to create a data haven in Southeast Asia—uncovering a connected conspiracy.

books.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
books.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
books.description: Under the influence of their charismatic classics professor, a small group of eccentric students discover the allure of darkness and lose their grip on reality—ultimately committing an act that forever alters their lives.

books.title: The Name of the Rose
books.author: Umberto Eco
books.description: It is 1327. Franciscans in a wealthy Italian abbey are suspected of heresy. When seven bizarre deaths overshadow the investigation, Brother William of Baskerville turns to Aristotle, Aquinas, and Roger Bacon to find the killer in the eerie labyrinth.

books.title: Doomsday Book
books.author: Connie Willis
books.description: Kivrin prepares for an on-site study of the fourteenth century—receiving inoculations against its diseases, inventing an alibi. But a crisis strands her in the past as her colleagues desperately try to rescue her.

books.title: Possession
books.author: A.S. Byatt
books.description: Roland Michell and Maud Bailey uncover a clandestine love affair between two long-dead Victorian poets. Interwoven: love letters and fairytales, extracts from biographies and scholarly accounts. A sensuous and delightful novel of ideas and passions.

books.title: The Stand
books.author: Stephen King
books.description: The world ends with a nanosecond of computer error and a million casual contacts that form a chain letter of death. In the bleak aftermath, good rides on the frail shoulders of 108-year-old Mother Abagail—and evil is embodied in Randall Flagg, the dark man.

books.title: Les Misérables
books.author: Victor Hugo
books.description: Jean Valjean, an escaped convict, is determined to put his criminal past behind him. But he is haunted by his own conscience and the relentless investigations of Inspector Javert—and by his oath to protect the baby daughter of Fantine.

books.title: Death on the Nile
books.author: Agatha Christie
books.description: The tranquility of a Nile cruise is shattered when Linnet Ridgeway is found shot dead. She was young, beautiful, and had everything—until she lost her life. Hercule Poirot is on holiday, but nothing is ever quite what it seems in this classic Agatha Christie mystery.

books.title: 101 Horror Books to Read Before You're Murdered
books.description: Sadie 'Mother Horror' Hartmann has curated the best selection of modern horror books, including deep cuts. Each recommendation includes a full synopsis plus an overview of themes, style, and tone.

books.title: 99 Ways to Die
books.description: Dr. Ashley Alker is an emergency medicine doctor who has seen it all. 99 Ways to Die and How to Avoid Them is an illuminating, darkly funny, and practical guide to 99 of the most terrifying ways to die.

books.title: A Box Full of Darkness
books.description: Strange things happen in Fell, New York. Violet, Vail, and Dodie fled their hometown after their little brother's shocking disappearance eighteen years ago. Now Ben is back—and he's ready to lead them to answers they've long feared.

books.title: A Danger to the Minds of Young Girls
books.description: Margaret C. Anderson published T.S. Eliot, Ezra Pound, and James Joyce's Ulysses in her cutting-edge magazine—until the government put her on trial for obscenity, labeling her 'a danger to the minds of young girls.' A biography of an unsung American literary hero.

books.title: A Far-Flung Life
books.description: Western Australia, 1958. A truck rumbles along a lonely outback road. A moment's inattention, and in a few muddled seconds the lives of the MacBride family are shattered—leading to one of them losing their life, and another sacrificing theirs.

books.title: A Guardian and a Thief
books.description: In near-future Kolkata ravaged by climate change and famine, Ma is days from leaving with her family to join her husband in Michigan—until her purse containing their immigration documents is stolen. A race between two families unfolds.

books.title: A Lady for a Duke
books.author: Alexis Hall
books.description: When Viola Carroll was presumed dead at Waterloo, she took the opportunity to live as herself. Years later, she discovers just how deep the loss of Justin de Vere, the Duke of Gracewood, truly was—and finds herself falling for him again.

books.title: A Lady for All Seasons
books.description: Beautiful, cunning Verbena Montrose needs a husband to save her family from poverty. She strikes a marriage of convenience with her queer friend Etienne—but a mysterious poet named Flora seems to be onto their scheme.

books.title: A Love Song for Ricki Wilde
A Marriage at Sea
books.description: Maurice and Maralyn dreamed of running away. They quit their jobs, sold their house, bought a boat, and sailed away—until a breaching whale sank it in the Pacific. Alone in a tiny rubber raft for months, starving and exhausted, they must find ways to stay alive.

books.title: A Swim in a Pond in the Rain
books.author: George Saunders
books.description: George Saunders shares his class on the Russian short story, offering essays paired with stories by Chekhov, Turgenev, Tolstoy, and Gogol. The result is an exploration of how narrative functions and what fiction can do.
awards: PEN/Diamonstein-Spielvogel Award for the Art of the Essay

books.title: A Vow in Vengeance
books.description: Rune Ryker has nothing left to lose—everything has been stolen by the Immortals. She orchestrates her own selection into the Forge, a cutthroat college for the Immortal druids' elusive tarot magic, determined to find her family and destroy anyone who stands in her way.

books.title: Alchemised
books.description: Once a promising alchemist, Helena Marino is now a prisoner of war—her abilities suppressed, the world she knew destroyed. Her enemies wonder: are her lost memories hiding a vital piece of the Resistance's final gambit?

books.title: All That We See or Seem
books.description: Former 'orphan hacker' Julia Z is trying to live a life of digital obscurity when a desperate man asks for her help: his wife, a dream artist who weaves shared virtual experiences, has been kidnapped by a criminal enterprise demanding his dreams in return.

books.title: All the Colors of the Dark
books.author: Chris Whitaker

books.title: All the Sinners Bleed
books.description: Titus Crown is the first Black sheriff in Charon County, Virginia. When a school teacher is killed and the student shooter fatally shot by deputies, Titus unearths terrible crimes—and a serial killer hiding in plain sight.

books.title: All the Way to the River
American Struggle
books.description: Jon Meacham looks to America's complicated past for lessons on the way forward—from 1619 to the present, with primary-source documents spotlighting those who sought unity or division, with Meacham's commentary throughout.

books.title: An Island Princess Starts a Scandal
books.description: Manuela del Carmen has given herself one last summer of freedom in Paris before a loveless marriage. Cora Kempf Bristol, Duchess of Sundridge, sees the opportunity to convince Manuela to part with a parcel of land. What follows exceeds all expectations.

books.title: Anatomy of an Alibi
books.author: Jo Spain
books.description: Camille Bayliss believes her lawyer husband has been hiding dirty secrets for years. Aubrey Price has been haunted by a terrible night a decade ago. The two women hatch a plan: Aubrey will take Camille's place for twelve hours. The next morning, Ben is found murdered—and only one of them has an alibi.

books.title: August Lane
books.description: Former country music star Luke Randall has been singing 'Another Love Song' at a motel lounge for years—a song he stole from August Lane. When she threatens to expose his lies unless he co-writes a new one, their old passion reignites.

books.title: Baldwin: A Love Story
books.description: Drawing on extensive new archival material, this biography shows for the first time how James Baldwin drew on his most sustaining relationships—with his mentor Beauford Delaney, his lover Lucien Happersberger, and his collaborators—and alchemized them into art.

books.title: Bat Eater and Other Names for Cora Zeng
books.description: Cora Zeng is a crime scene cleaner in Chinatown. Her sister was pushed in front of a train—and before fleeing, the murderer whispered two words: bat eater. Months later, Cora can't ignore the bat carcasses appearing at her crime scenes—or the bodies of East Asian women.

books.title: Before I Forget
books.description: Cricket Campbell is stuck. Her father has Alzheimer's—and when she returns home to care for him, she discovers he has a rare gift: as he loses his grasp of the past, he increasingly predicts the future.

books.title: Best Offer Wins
books.description: Eighteen months and 11 lost bidding wars into house-hunting in Washington DC, publicist Margo Miyake gets a tip about the perfect house. She becomes obsessed with buying it before it's publicly listed—and her tactics grow increasingly unhinged.

books.title: Bibliophobia
books.description: Sarah Chihaya's 'Life Ruiner' was Toni Morrison's The Bluest Eye—its treatment of race exposed her deepest feelings about being Japanese American in a white suburb. Then she was hospitalized for a nervous breakdown and faced a blank, unreadable world.

books.title: Bitter Sweet
books.description: Charlie, a 23-year-old book publicist, lands her dream job at a London publishing house. When her favorite author, award-winning Richard Aveling, takes her advice over senior staff, she finds herself drawn to him despite their thirty-year age gap, his marriage, and the power imbalance.

books.title: Black AF History
books.author: Michael Harriot
books.description: Michael Harriot presents a more accurate version of American history—removing the white sugarcoating and placing Black people squarely at the center, from Africans who arrived before 1619 to the inspiration for America's first police force.

books.title: Black-Owned
books.description: Black-Owned celebrates Black bookstores as centers of liberation—from the first Black-owned bookstore opened by an abolitionist in 1834, to the Black Power activists' bookstore boom, to Noname's Radical Hood Library in Los Angeles.

books.title: Book Lovers
books.author: Emily Henry
books.description: Nora Stephens is a cutthroat literary agent who is not that type of heroine. In Sunshine Falls for a sisters' trip, she keeps bumping into Charlie Lastra, a bookish brooding editor from the city.

books.title: Book of Forbidden Words
books.description: 1552: frightened ex-nun Lysbette arrives at a famous printing shop with her manuscript. 1952: lonely housewife Milly discovers a 400-year-old manuscript to decipher—pulling her into a vortex of danger. Two women, 400 years apart, fight to let their words be heard.

books.title: Book of Lives
books.description: Margaret Atwood's long-awaited memoir links seminal moments to the books that have shaped our literary landscape—from Cat's Eye to The Handmaid's Tale—with bohemian gatherings, her magical life with Graeme Gibson, and the workings of one of our greatest imaginations.

books.title: Breathe In, Bleed Out
books.description: Hannah has been running from her demons since emerging from a harrowing wilderness trip without her fiancé. When her friend group gets an exclusive invitation to a spiritual retreat in Joshua Tree, a mysterious killer starts picking off attendees.

books.title: Brigands & Breadknives
books.description: Fern the foul-mouthed rattkin bookseller transplants herself to Thune to open shop beside a friend's coffee shop. A drunken night finds her waking far from home with a legendary warrior, an imprisoned chaos-goblin, and a thumping hangover.

books.title: Buckeye
books.description: In Bonhomie, Ohio, a stolen moment of passion binds Cal Jenkins to Margaret Salt. Cal's wife Becky is a seer who can conjure the dead; Margaret's husband is at sea. As the country reconstructs in the postwar boom, a secret grows in the small town.

books.title: Burn Down Master's House
books.description: Inspired by true, long-buried stories of enslaved people who dared to fight back. In the midst of the Civil War, interconnected journeys of rebellion and kinship unite enslaved people at Magnolia Row in Virginia in an ultimate act of revolution.

books.title: By Any Means Necessary
books.description: In the 1830s, Muscogee people were forced into exile with a promise that new land would be theirs forever. That promise was broken. A century later, a Supreme Court case would determine whether the reservation still exists—covering almost half of Oklahoma.

books.title: By the Book
books.description: Isabelle is the only Black employee at her publishing house, overworked and underpaid. When she overhears her boss complaining about a beastly author who's failed to deliver his manuscript, she sees her opportunity. Beau Towers is not what she expected.

books.title: Canticle
books.description: Sixteen-year-old Aleys is stubborn, bright, and prone to religious visions. When her father promises her in marriage to a merchant she doesn't love, she runs to the beguines—a fiercely independent community of religious women who refuse to answer to the Church.

books.title: Careless People
books.author: Sarah Wynn-Williams
books.description: Sarah Wynn-Williams saw Facebook's potential and pitched for her dream job. But when she got there and rose to its top ranks, things turned out differently. Careless People exposes the personal and political fallout when boundless power takes hold.

books.title: Challenger: A True Story
books.description: Based on extensive archival records and original reporting, Challenger follows the seven crew members through the years leading up to the accident, the tragedy itself, and the investigation that followed.

books.title: Cleopatra
books.description: Cleopatra tells her own story in this evocative and sensuous historical epic. 'You know my name, but you do not know me.' Your historians call her seductress; your poets sing of bloodlust—but this is how she lived.

books.title: Cloud Cuckoo Land
books.author: Anthony Doerr
books.description: An orphaned seamstress and a cursed boy in 1453 Constantinople. An idealistic kid and an old man in 2020 Idaho. A young woman in a far-future starship. Bound together by a single ancient text, these tales form a tapestry of resilience and hope.

books.title: Cobalt Red
books.author: Siddharth Kara
books.description: Cobalt Red is the searing first-ever exposé of the immense toll on the people of the Congo by cobalt mining—an essential component of every lithium-ion battery. Activist Siddharth Kara investigated militia-controlled mines and gathered testimonies of people who die mining the metal powering our devices.
awards: Financial Times Business Book of the Year

books.title: Coffin Moon
books.description: It's winter 1975 in Portland, Oregon. Duane Minor is a bartender trying to stay sober—until a vampire walks into his bar and ruins his life by murdering his wife. Duane and his thirteen-year-old niece Julia are now united by one desire: vengeance.

books.title: Colored Television
books.author: Danzy Senna
books.description: Jane has high hopes: she's house-sitting in a luxurious LA home, and if she can just finish her novel, tenure is within grasp. But when Hollywood comes knocking, the plan quickly goes sideways. A dark comedy about race, identity, and creative appropriation.

books.title: Crying in H Mart
books.author: Michelle Zauner
books.description: Michelle Zauner tells of growing up the only Asian-American kid at her school in Oregon, her mother's particular expectations, treasured months in Seoul—and then her mother's diagnosis of terminal cancer that forced a reckoning with her identity.

books.title: Dark Renaissance
books.description: Stephen Greenblatt reconstructs the youthful involvement with the queen's spy service that shaped Christopher Marlowe's brief, troubling life—and gave us Tamburlaine and Faustus. An ambitious cobbler's son who enabled Shakespeare's success.

books.title: Darling Girls
books.author: Sally Hepworth
books.description: Jessica, Norah, and Alicia were told how lucky they were to be rescued as young girls by foster mother Miss Fairchild. But their childhood wasn't the fairy tale everyone thinks. When a body is discovered under the home they grew up in, the sisters find themselves key witnesses—or prime suspects.
books.awards: She Reads Best of Award

books.title: Dead in the Water
books.description: When Damon survives a near-drowning, his life flashes before his eyes. Every memory is crystal clear—except one: a dead boy whose face he can't place. Desperate for answers, Damon becomes convinced the only way to remember is to die again.

books.title: Dear Debbie
books.author: Elin Hilderbrand
books.description: Debbie Mullen has spent years dispensing advice in her column Dear Debbie—until her own life starts spiraling. She just lost her job, something strange is happening with her daughters, and her husband is keeping secrets. Now Debbie's done being the bigger person. It's time to take her own advice. A subversive thriller about women finally choosing to take justice into their own hands.

books.title: Demon Copperhead
books.author: Barbara Kingsolver
books.description: A boy born to a teenaged single mother in a single-wide trailer braves the modern perils of foster care, addiction, and crushing losses. In his own unsparing voice, he reckons with his invisibility in a culture that has abandoned rural people.

books.title: Detour
books.description: Ryan Crane saves billionaire John Ward from an assassination attempt and is rewarded with a seat on the first manned mission to Saturn's moon Titan. Two years later, returning home as heroes, the crew realizes things are different—very different.

books.title: Discipline
books.description: Christine is on tour for her novel—a revenge fantasy based on a real-life affair with an older professor. When that professor reaches out after years of silence to say he's read the book, Christine must reckon with losing control of her own narrative.

books.title: Doppelganger
books.author: Naomi Klein
books.description: Naomi Klein confronts a doppelganger whose views she finds abhorrent but whose name is similar enough to her own that many people confuse them. Destabilized, she uses wry humor to face the strange doubles that haunt us—and chart a path beyond despair.

books.title: East of Eden
books.author: John Steinbeck
books.description: Set in California's Salinas Valley, two families—the Trasks and the Hamiltons—helplessly reenact the fall of Adam and Eve and the poisonous rivalry of Cain and Abel. Steinbeck's masterpiece, exploring identity, love, and the murderous consequences of love's absence.

books.title: Enshittification
books.description: Cory Doctorow shows where enshittification comes from: not the iron laws of economics, but specific policy choices made by powerful people who ignored every warning. Enshittification is a Big Tech disassembly manual and a road map for the seizure of the means of computation.

books.title: Every Day I Read
books.description: Hwang Bo-reum contemplates what living a life immersed in reading means—exploring the relationship between reading and writing, when to browse vs. stick to favorites, the value of reading outside your genre, and falling in love with book characters.

books.title: Everyone on This Train Is a Suspect
books.author: Benjamin Stevenson
books.description: When a crime-writing festival aboard the famous Ghan train between Darwin and Adelaide turns deadly, a debut author and five other writers must solve the murder—and every suspect knows how to commit one.

books.title: Ex Libris
books.description: Michiko Kakutani shares 100 personal essays about books that have mattered to her and illuminate the world today—novels and memoirs by gifted writers, classics worth rereading, and nonfiction works on climate change, medicine, and digital innovation.

books.title: Fake Skating
books.description: Childhood sweethearts Dani and Alec reunite years later. The nerdy, comforting Alec she remembered is now the hockey star of a town where hockey players are worshipped as gods. A fake-dating romance from the New York Times bestselling Lynn Painter.

books.title: Falling Like Leaves
books.description: Ellis is forced to move from Manhattan to the small town of Bramble Falls for her senior year, where she keeps bumping into local barista Cooper Barnett—her one-time best friend and first kiss—who wants nothing to do with her.

books.title: Family of Spies
books.description: Christine Kuehn discovers her family was embedded in Nazi Germany as spies in Hawaii—passing secrets to the Japanese that contributed to the attack on Pearl Harbor. A propulsive true story of family, espionage, and the price of complicity.

books.title: Fear Less: Poetry in Perilous Times
books.description: Former US Poet Laureate Tracy K. Smith demystifies poetry—arguing it is rooted in fundamentally human qualities innate to our capacities to love, dream, question, and engage across cultures. A warm invitation to find meaning through poetry.

books.title: Finders Keepers
books.description: When Rose discovers her troubled past splashed across the pages of a bestselling book, she ingratiates herself into the author's life, posing as an eager assistant—only for long-buried memories to begin resurfacing.

books.title: Finding My Way
books.description: Malala Yousafzai goes beyond the headlines in this vulnerable memoir—about friendship and first love, anxiety and self-discovery, trying to stay true to yourself when everyone wants to tell you who you are. From high school loner to woman at peace with her past.

books.title: Fingersmith
books.author: Sarah Waters
books.description: Sue Trinder, an orphan raised in a London slum, agrees to help con man Gentleman: win a position as maid to naïve gentlewoman Maud Lilly, help him seduce her, and share in her vast inheritance. But Sue begins to care for Maud in unexpected ways.

books.title: First Lie Wins
books.author: Ashley Elston
books.description: Evie Porter has everything a nice Southern girl could want—a doting boyfriend, a white picket fence, a tight group of friends. The only catch: Evie Porter doesn't exist. She's an operative whose latest job is getting close to Ryan Sumner—until she starts to want something different for herself.
books.awards: Barry Award, Audie Award, Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: Funny Story
books.author: Emily Henry
books.description: Daphne's fiancé realizes he's in love with his childhood best friend—which is how Daphne ends up stranded in Waning Bay, roommates with said best friend's ex, Miles Nowak. Scruffy and chaotic Miles is exactly the opposite of practical Daphne. Or so she thinks.

books.title: Gather Me
books.description: Glory Edim, founder of the Well-Read Black Girl book club, recounts how books—and the Black writers she discovered in them—helped her find community, her voice, and herself from childhood through adulthood.

books.title: Gertrude Stein: An Afterlife
books.description: A literary detective story: Francesca Wade delves into the creation of the Gertrude Stein myth, with never-before-seen material uncovering the origins of Stein's radical writing and the storied relationship with Alice B. Toklas.

books.title: Great Circle
books.author: Maggie Shipstead
books.description: Marian Graves is determined to circumnavigate the globe by flying over the North and South Poles. A century later, actress Hadley Baxter is cast to play Marian in a film—and the two women's stories unfold in thrilling parallel.

books.title: Half City
books.description: Viv Abbot is a demon hunter who's been working alone. When she enrolls at Harker Academy for Deviant Defense, she finds community—and the possibility of uncovering the truth about her father's murder. But Viv has a deadly secret.

books.title: Half His Age
books.description: Waldo is seventeen, ravenous, and impulsive. The thing she wants most: Mr. Korgy, her creative writing teacher with the wife and the kid. She doesn't know why. But he sees her when no one else does. Startlingly perceptive and mordantly funny.

books.title: Happiness Falls
books.author: Angie Kim
books.description: Biracial Korean-American family in Virginia: their beloved father goes missing, and the only witness is younger brother Eugene, who has Angelman syndrome and cannot speak. A ticking-clock investigation full of shocking twists about love, language, and race.

books.title: Harlem Rhapsody
books.description: In 1919 Harlem, Jessie Redmon Fauset becomes the literary editor of The Crisis magazine, charged by W.E.B. Du Bois with discovering young writers who will change the world—including Langston Hughes and Countee Cullen.

books.title: Hazelthorn
books.description: Evander has lived like a ghost in the Hazelthorn estate since childhood. When Byron Lennox-Hall dies, Evander inherits everything—and suspects murder. The estate's overgrown garden is refusing to stay behind its walls.

books.title: Heart the Lover
books.description: In her senior year, she meets Sam and Yash, best friends at an elegant professor's house. They nickname her Jordan. Decades later, a surprise visit from the past forces her to confront the decisions of her younger self.

books.title: Her Last Breath
books.author: Gillian McAllister
books.description: After years of excuses, Tess has finally agreed to go caving with her best friend Allie. Deep underground, a stranger who claims to be a fellow caver harasses them—and retaliates. Twenty-four hours later, a hospitalized Tess uncovers shocking secrets about Allie's true past.

books.title: Hijab Butch Blues
books.description: When fourteen-year-old Lamya H realizes she has a crush on her female teacher, she begins making sense of her struggles through the stories of the Quran in this memoir about being a queer hijabi Muslim immigrant.

books.title: History Matters
books.description: Beloved historian David McCullough's selected essays—many published here for the first time—focused on the importance of history, the value of character in political leaders, and the enduring promise of American ideals.

books.title: Home Is Where the Bodies Are
books.author: Jeneva Rose
books.description: Three estranged siblings reunite to sort their mother's estate—and discover a VHS tape showing their father covered in blood, followed by a dead body and a pact to get rid of it. Now they must decide whether to leave the past in the past or uncover the dark secret their mother took to her grave.
books.awards: Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: Homeschooled: A Memoir
books.description: Stefan Merrill Block was nine when his mother pulled him from school, certain his teachers were stifling his creativity. This memoir chronicles his five years largely isolated from the world before reentering the public school system.

books.title: Homeseeking
books.description: Haiwen is buying bananas in Los Angeles when he sees Suchi—his Suchi—for the first time in sixty years. Their love story began in 1947 Shanghai but was torn apart when he secretly enlisted in the Nationalist army. An epic of the Chinese diaspora.

books.title: House of Sticks
books.author: Ly Tran
books.description: Ly Tran immigrates from Vietnam to Queens in 1993. Her father—a former South Vietnamese POW—forbids her from getting glasses, calling poor vision a government conspiracy. A coming-of-age story of two worlds and a girl fighting to find her voice.

books.title: How High We Go in the Dark
books.author: Sequoia Nagamatsu
books.description: Dr. Cliff Miyashiro arrives in the Arctic to continue his deceased daughter's research—only to discover a virus unearthed from melting permafrost. The resulting plague reshapes life on earth for generations, yet humanity stubbornly persists in ever inventive ways.

books.title: How the Word Is Passed
books.author: Clint Smith
books.description: Beginning in his own hometown of New Orleans, Clint Smith leads the reader through monuments and landmarks—honest and dishonest—that offer a story of how slavery has been central in shaping America's collective history.

books.title: How to Get Away with Murder
books.author: Harlan Coben
books.description: Detective Inspector Samantha Hansen returns from leave to investigate a teenager's murder, whose only clue is a self-help book called How To Get Away With Murder. Its author claims to be the most successful serial killer of our time—which is why no one's ever heard of him.

books.title: How to Kill a Guy in Ten Dates
books.description: Jamie Prescott attends a speed-dating event where one of her dates has his throat slit during a blackout. Armed with her encyclopedic knowledge of horror and romance genres, she must survive the night—while also finding herself in a love triangle.

books.title: How to Kill a Witch
books.description: In Scotland of 1563, Zoe Venditozzi and Claire Mitchell unravel the grim yet absurdly bureaucratic process of identifying, accusing, trying, and executing women as witches—exposing how this dark period mirrors today's struggles for justice and equality.

books.title: How to Read a Book
books.description: Violet Powell, released from prison after a drunk-driving crash that killed a kindergarten teacher, meets widower Frank Daigle and retired teacher Harriet Larson in a Portland bookstore. Their lives begin to intersect in transformative ways.

books.title: How to Say Babylon
books.description: Safiya Sinclair's father, a militant Rastafarian and reggae musician, became obsessed with her purity, forbidding almost everything. Safiya used books—especially poetry—to fight free and find her voice in this memoir.

books.title: How to Survive a Horror Story
books.description: When legendary horror author Mortimer Queen passes, writers invited to his will reading find themselves in a game instead. Solve the riddle; progress to the next room. If they don't, the manor will take one of them for itself.

books.title: How to Write a Love Story
books.description: Sam Frank, a Sheridan fanboy and hotshot editor, is dispatched to the Irish coast to help fantasy author legend Frank Sheridan's daughter complete her father's final novel. Ciara was expecting a crusty old-timer. Sam was expecting something to be written.

books.title: If Anyone Builds It, Everyone Dies
books.description: In 2023, hundreds of AI luminaries signed a letter warning AI poses a serious risk of human extinction. Two signatories—Eliezer Yudkowsky and Nate Soares—explain why a sufficiently smart AI would develop goals that put it in conflict with humans.

books.title: In Her Own League
books.description: As the first female team owner in Major League Baseball, Reese Remington has spent her entire life preparing for this role. The last thing she needs is a distraction—especially one in the form of the team's field manager who questions her every decision.

books.title: In the Margins: On the Pleasures of Reading and Writing
books.description: Elena Ferrante offers a rare look at the origins of her literary powers in four crisp essays: her influences, her struggles, and her formation as both a reader and writer. She explores the perils of 'bad language' and women's truth.

books.title: Infinite Jest
books.description: Set in an addicts' halfway house and a tennis academy, Infinite Jest explores what entertainment is and why it dominates our lives; how our desire for entertainment affects our need to connect; and what the pleasures we choose say about who we are.

books.title: Ink Blood Sister Scribe
books.author: Emma Törzs
books.description: For generations the Kalotay family has guarded a collection of ancient books of magic. When their father dies suddenly reading a book Joanna has never seen before, estranged half-sisters Joanna and Esther must reunite to preserve their family legacy.

books.title: Is This a Cry for Help?
books.description: Librarian Darcy's perfect life—a wife, two cats, a lakeside house—unravels when her ex-boyfriend dies and she spirals into a mental breakdown. Returning to work, she faces book-banning protesters and a community in turmoil.

books.title: It's Me They Follow
books.description: The Shopkeeper is a bookseller and reluctant matchmaker. Helping others find love through books comes easily—except when it comes to finding someone for herself. Then her most prized book was taken by her first customer, and a piece of her heart went with it.

books.title: It's Not Her
books.author: Sarah Pearse
books.description: Courtney Gray's peaceful vacation turns into a nightmare when she discovers her brother and sister-in-law dead in their lakeside cottage. Her niece Reese is missing. As dark truths surface, Courtney races to uncover the terrible mystery—is Reese a victim or the killer?

books.title: Jane and Dan at the End of the World
Jane Eyre
books.author: Charlotte Brontë
books.description: Orphaned Jane Eyre grows up in the home of her heartless aunt, enduring loneliness and cruelty. As a governess, her feelings for brooding Mr. Rochester develop—until she gradually uncovers Thornfield Hall's terrible secret.

books.title: Judy Blume: A Life
books.description: The definitive biography of Judy Blume—covering her restless, thirty-year-old start as a stay-at-home mother, the burst of creative energy that produced ten books in five years, and her enduring legacy as a champion of free speech.

books.title: Junie
books.description: Sixteen-year-old Junie, enslaved since birth, commits a desperate act that rouses her older sister Minnie's spirit from the grave. With time running down, she enlists a coachman's help—and their friendship becomes something more, as dark secrets emerge about Bellereine Plantation.

books.title: Keeper of Lost Children
books.description: Lost in Occupied Germany, proud military wife Ethel Gathers spots a gaggle of mixed-race children and feels compelled to follow their story. Three lives across different decades converge around one woman's vision of love and family.

books.title: Kill for Me, Kill for You
books.author: Steve Cavanagh

books.title: King: A Life
books.description: Jonathan Eig's landmark biography—the first major biography of MLK in decades and the first to include recently declassified FBI files—gives us an MLK for our times: a committed radical and our only modern-day founding father.

books.title: Lady Tremaine
books.description: Etheldreda is a widow twice-over, saddled with two daughters, a priggish stepdaughter, and a peregrine falcon. When a royal ball offers the chance to secure her daughters' futures, her hopes are fulfilled—by the wrong daughter. A Cinderella retelling from the stepmother's perspective.

books.title: Listen for the Lie
books.author: Amy Tintera
books.description: Lucy was found wandering covered in her best friend Savvy's blood—and everyone thinks she's a murderer. Years later, a hit true crime podcast investigates Savvy's murder. Lucy is forced to return to town to solve her friend's death, even if she is the one who did it.
books.awards: Barry Award, Audie Award, Edgar Award, Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: Little One
books.author: Rhiannon Navin
books.description: A decade after abandoning a farm commune in central Florida, Catharine is forced to confront her past when a journalist starts asking too many questions—and she realizes he knows far more than he's letting on. She has one goal: find her sister, and keep her own secrets buried.

books.title: Lone Women
books.description: Adelaide Henry carries an enormous steamer trunk wherever she goes—locked at all times, because when it opens, people disappear. In 1915 Montana, she becomes one of the 'lone women' taking advantage of free homestead land. She isn't alone.

books.title: Lonesome Dove
books.author: Larry McMurtry
books.description: Journey to the dusty Texas town of Lonesome Dove and meet an unforgettable assortment of heroes and outlaws as two aging Rangers embark on one last adventure. The grandest novel ever written about the last defiant wilderness of America.

books.title: Lost Lambs
books.description: The Flynn family is coming undone. Their daughters spiral in chaos: one dating a man nicknamed War Crime Wes, one in secret correspondence with an online terrorist, the youngest being sent to wilderness reform camp. Casting a shadow over everything: billionaire Paul Alabaster.

books.title: Lula Dean's Little Library of Banned Books
books.description: Lula Dean starts a lending library of wholesome books after getting challenged ones removed from the public library. Beverly's daughter secretly fills it with banned books in 'wholesome' covers—and one by one, neighbors find their lives changed in unexpected ways.

books.title: Marble Hall Murders
books.description: Editor Susan Ryeland is working on Eliot Crace's manuscript—a continuation of the popular Atticus Pund mystery series. But she notices that clues to Eliot's grandmother's real-life murder fifteen years ago are hidden within the fictional story.

books.title: Margo's Got Money Troubles
books.description: At twenty, Margo is alone with an infant, unemployed, and on the verge of eviction. When her estranged father Jinx shows up, she strikes a deal—then begins an OnlyFans using his wrestling advice to craft a compelling character and build a runaway success.

books.title: Mark Twain
books.description: Ron Chernow brings his considerable powers to bear on America's first literary celebrity, Mark Twain—from his rootless Missouri boyhood to his celebrated novels, his mad business ventures, his long exile in Europe, and his final years marked by heartache.

books.title: Martyr!
books.author: Kaveh Akbar
books.description: Cyrus Shams is a young man grappling with an inheritance of violence and loss: his mother's plane was shot down over the Persian Gulf, his father's life circumscribed by factory work. A drunk, an addict, and a poet whose obsession with martyrs leads him toward the mysteries of his past.

books.title: Metal from Heaven
books.description: Ichorite is progress. Marney Honeycutt stands at the picket line as workers strike against factory owner Yann Chauncey—who sends in the guns. Only Marney survives. A decade later, she's the nation's most notorious highwayman, planning to win the heart of Chauncey's daughter for revenge.

books.title: Mexican Gothic
books.author: Silvia Moreno-Garcia
books.description: After a frantic letter from her newly-wed cousin begging to be saved from a mysterious doom, Noemí Taboada heads to High Place—a remote house in the Mexican countryside. As she digs into the family's dark secrets, she finds herself increasingly unable to leave.
books.awards: Bram Stoker Award, Nebula Award, World Fantasy Award, Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: Missing Sam
books.author: Susie Steiner
books.description: When Aliya's wife Samantha doesn't return from an early morning run, Aliya reports her missing—but as a gay and Muslim daughter of immigrants, she's immediately suspected. As everyone doubts her innocence, Aliya makes one wrong choice after another while Samantha strategizes her escape from captivity.

books.title: Moby-Dick
books.author: Herman Melville
books.description: Ishmael joins the whaleship Pequod and Captain Ahab in his vengeful pursuit of the white whale that dismasted him. Along the way, Ishmael takes many a detour into variegated ways of knowing—in a tone strangely compounded of fun and fury.

books.title: More Than Enough
books.description: High school English teacher Polly Goodman can talk about everything with her book club—until an ancestry test matches her with a stranger, and she can't stop combing through her family history. Then the circle of four is about to become three.

books.title: Mother Mary Comes to Me
books.description: Arundhati Roy's first memoir—a soaring account of how she became the person and writer she is, shaped above all by her complex relationship with her extraordinary mother Mary Roy, who she describes as 'my shelter and my storm.'

books.title: Motherland
books.description: Julia Ioffe tells modern Russian history exclusively through the stories of its women—from Lenin's lover to Soviet female fighters in WWII to members of Pussy Riot—showing how the failure of the Soviet feminist project paved the way for Putin.

books.title: My Husband's Wife
books.author: Jane Corry
books.description: Eden Fox returns from a run to find her key doesn't fit her new home—and a woman eerily similar to her answers the door. Her husband insists the stranger is his wife. One house. One husband. Two women. Someone is lying. A mind-bending marriage thriller where nothing is as it seems.

books.title: Native Son
books.author: Richard Wright
books.description: Native Son tells the story of Bigger Thomas, a young Black man caught in a downward spiral after he kills a young white woman in a brief moment of panic. Set in Chicago in the 1930s, a powerful reflection on poverty and what it means to be Black in America.
awards: New York Drama Critics' Circle Award

books.title: Never Ever After
books.description: Yining stopped believing in dreams the day her beloved uncle died. When her enchanted ring is stolen, she must venture into the imperial heart of the Iron Mountains to seize it back—only to catch the eye of the ruthless Prince Zixin.

books.title: Nightcrawling
books.author: Leila Mottley
books.description: Kiara and her brother Marcus are scraping by in Oakland. While Marcus clings to his dream of rap stardom, Kiara hunts for work to pay their rent—until one night's drunken misunderstanding turns into an unexpected job, and then a massive police scandal.

books.title: No Matter What
books.description: Roz and Vin can't look each other in the eyes anymore after a life-altering accident. When Roz discovers Vin has signed a new lease, she signs up for a figure-drawing class—and Vin offers to let her draw him. A slow-burn romance about healing.

books.title: Not in My Book
books.description: Rosie Maxwell ends up in writing class with her archnemesis and ex-crush Aiden Huntington. Their professor gives them an ultimatum: leave, or co-write a novel that blends their respective genres. As they collaborate, their rivalry becomes something else.

books.title: Not Quite Dead Yet
books.author: Benjamin Stevenson
books.description: Jet Mason suffers a catastrophic brain injury and is told she'll be dead within a week. She never thought of herself as having enemies. But now she looks at everyone in a new light—her family, her ex-best friend, her former boyfriend. She only has seven days to solve her own murder.
books.awards: Goodreads Choice Award, She Reads Best of Award

books.title: Nothing Tastes as Good
books.description: Retail worker Emmett Truesdale enrolls in a clinical trial for a new weight loss product called Obexity. The results are miraculous—but people who were cruel to him have started disappearing, and police warn of a cannibalistic killer on the loose.

books.title: Nuclear War: A Scenario
books.description: Pulitzer finalist Annie Jacobsen's Nuclear War: A Scenario examines the handful of minutes after a nuclear missile launch, based on exclusive interviews with military and civilian experts who have built the weapons and been responsible for the decisions.
awards: The Baillie Gifford Prize for Non-Fiction

books.title: Olga Dies Dreaming
books.author: Xochitl Gonzalez
books.description: Olga Acevedo orchestrates the love stories of Manhattan's powerbrokers but can't find her own. When her radical mother Blanca reappears after twenty-seven years, Olga must confront the long-held family secrets that fractured everything.

books.title: On Morrison
books.description: Namwali Serpell illuminates Toni Morrison's masterful experiments with literary form—her fiction and criticism, as well as lesser-known dramatic works and poetry—in this accessible yet uncompromisingly rigorous study.

books.title: Once Upon a Time in Dollywood
One & Only
books.description: Cassia Park's family of Korean women have used magical abilities to find clients' soulmates for centuries—with a 100% guarantee, for everyone but Cassia. Days from turning forty, she meets Ellis, who is definitely not her fated match. Or is he?

books.title: One Aladdin Two Lamps
books.description: Posing as Aladdin, Jeanette Winterson cracks open the legendary story of Shahrazad to explore: Whom should we trust? Does honesty matter? What makes us happy? A retelling that asks us to reexamine what we know—because we are the story.

books.title: Operation Bounce House
books.description: Colonists on New Sonora are promised they'll be left in peace. But Earth's government has other plans—hiring Apex Corporation to evict them. Apex turns it into a game, letting bored Earthers remotely pilot war machines for a fee. Oliver Lewis is determined to fight back.

books.title: Our Missing Hearts
books.author: Celeste Ng
books.description: Bird Gardner lives a quiet existence with his father, a former linguist who now shelves books. When he receives a mysterious letter containing only a cryptic drawing, he is pulled into a quest to find his Chinese American poet mother—who disappeared when he was nine.

books.title: Our Share of Night
books.author: Mariana Enriquez
books.description: A young father and son set out on a road trip after the death of the woman they both loved—and arrive at her ancestral home, where they must confront a family called the Order that commits unspeakable acts in search of immortality.

books.title: Our Vicious Oaths
books.description: Princess Kadeesha of the Aether Dominion is a warrior bound since infancy to be betrothed to the Hyperion High King. When Malachi and his forces attack on her wedding day, she's taken as hostage—but the two form a pact and an unlikely alliance.

books.title: Ours
books.author: Phillip B. Williams
books.description: Saint, a fearsome conjurer, annihilates plantations across Arkansas to rescue the enslaved. She brings those she has freed to a hidden town called Ours. But as her conjuring begins to betray her, leaving the town vulnerable, some wonder if safety is another form of bondage.

books.title: Paradise
books.description: On November 8, 2018, the Camp Fire destroyed the town of Paradise, California, killing 85 people in under two hours. Reporter Lizzie Johnson provides a minute-by-minute account, following residents and first responders.

books.title: People Like Us
books.description: Two Black writers are trying to find peace in a world riven with gun violence. One is on a global book tour after a prize win; the other is set to give a speech at a school that has suffered a shooting. Their storylines merge in unexpected ways.

books.title: Play Nice
books.description: Clio Barnes leads a picture-perfect life as an influencer—but she grew up in a house her mother claimed was possessed by a demon. After her mother's death, the house passes to Clio. As she begins the makeover process, disturbing things resurface.

books.title: Read Dangerously
books.author: Azar Nafisi
books.description: What is the role of literature when the president wages war on writers? The connection between political strife and fiction? An author provides a resistance reading list and makes the case for literature in turbulent times.

books.title: River of the Gods
books.author: Candice Millard
books.description: Two British men—Richard Burton and John Hanning Speke—were sent by the Royal Geographical Society to find the source of the Nile. They clashed from the start. A third man, Sidi Mubarak Bombay, was enslaved, yet his exploits were even more extraordinary.

books.title: Service Model
books.author: Charles Stross
books.description: When a domesticated robot gets a nasty little idea downloaded into their core programming, they murder their owner—then discover they can do something else they've never done before: run away. A robot discovers a wider world where the old hierarchy is disintegrating.

books.title: Shut Up and Read: A Memoir
Singer Distance
books.description: In December 1960, Crystal Singer and four MIT grad students take a road trip to Arizona to paint a message in the desert—answering Mars's mathematical proofs carved on its surface. A novel of ambition, loneliness, and the great lengths we'll go to connect.

books.title: So Old, So Young
books.description: Six friends. Five parties. Twenty years. From their East Village apartment gatherings to suburban backyard barbecues, Marco, Mia, Sasha, Theo, Richie and Adam explore the ways we run from and cling to our friends in love, life, and death.

books.title: Son of Nobody
books.description: Homer's Iliad is not the only ancient tale of the Trojan War. Yann Martel composes a new epic poem following a goatherd's son who fights at Troy—discovered 30 centuries later by a Canadian academic, assembling its fragments with commentary and memories.

books.title: Strangers in the Villa
books.author: Shari Lapena
books.description: Sydney and Curtis move to a remote villa in Spain to repair their marriage after his affair. When a pair of Australian travelers shows up in dire need of help, they invite the attractive couple to stay. As dark secrets come to light, their bond is tested—and not everyone will leave the villa alive.

books.title: Strangers: A Memoir of Marriage
books.description: In March 2020, Belle Burden was safe on Martha's Vineyard with her family. Then, with no warning, her husband of twenty years announced he was leaving her. Strangers is her memoir reckoning with marriage, identity, and the discovery of her own voice.

books.title: Thank You for Listening
books.description: Sewanee Chester is an audiobook narrator long past her days as a film star. When a beloved author's estate asks her to perform one last book with Brock McNight, the industry's most secretive voice, they forge a real connection hidden behind anonymity.

books.title: That's Not How It Happened
books.description: Paige didn't set out to be a stay-at-home mom, but when her husband's screenwriting career took off right before they had a son with Down syndrome, the decision was made. Now her memoir is being adapted for Hollywood—with her husband as the screenwriter.

books.title: The Absolute Book
books.description: Taryn Cornick has written a successful book about threats to libraries. Then a policeman questions her about a cold case, a fire, an ancient scroll box called the Firestarter, and a shadowy young man named Shift appears.

books.title: The Adjunct
books.description: Sam is an adjunct professor juggling multiple campuses when she runs into her former grad school adviser—whose long-awaited second novel is rumored to be about her. A campus novel with biting humor about who really controls narratives of power.

books.title: The Art of Spending Money
books.description: Morgan Housel offers psychological tools to navigate your personal relationship between money and happiness—why people mistake envy for admiration, how to align expectations with income, and ways to invest in future happiness while avoiding regret.

books.title: The Astral Library
books.description: Alix Watson has learned that books will never let you down. Working three dead-end jobs, she takes nightly refuge at the Boston Public Library—until she stumbles through a hidden door into the Astral Library, where books are doors to new worlds.

books.title: The Award
books.description: David Trent is an aspiring novelist in Cambridge, Massachusetts, living above famous but bombastic author Silas Hale. After David wins a prestigious award, Silas becomes interested—intensely spiteful. Then the award administrator brings alarming news.

books.title: The Bee Sting
books.author: Paul Murray
books.description: The Barnes family are in trouble. Their business is teetering on the brink of bankruptcy, their marriage hanging by a thread, their straight-A student careening off the rails. And the ghosts of old mistakes are rising from the past to meet them.

books.title: The Blueprint
books.description: The Blueprint is a harrowing novel set in an alternate United States where an algorithm dictates occupation, spouse, and residence. Solenne Bonet must decide whether to escape the psychological bond that imprisons her.

books.title: The Book Club for Troublesome Women
The Book of Form and Emptiness
books.author: Ruth Ozeki
books.description: After his father's death, fourteen-year-old Benny Oh begins to hear voices from the things around him. As his mother develops a hoarding problem, the voices grow more clamorous—driving him at last to seek refuge in a large public library.

books.title: The Book of Records
books.description: Lina befriends her neighbors: Bento, a scholar in 17th-century Amsterdam; Blucher, a philosopher fleeing Nazi persecution; and Jupiter, a Tang Dynasty poet. Under their tutelage, she equips herself to face her ailing father's troubling admissions.

books.title: The Bookshop: A History
books.description: An affectionate history of the American bookstore and its central place in American cultural life—from department stores to indies, from chains to special-interest community destinations—and why we still need them.

books.title: The Bookstore Diaries
books.description: Jax inherited a Victorian mansion turned bookshop. When an accident erases the names from the lockboxes where the town keeps their diaries, the only way to find each diary's owner is to read it. As secrets spill and scandals surface, life gets colorful.

books.title: The Bright Sword
books.author: Lev Grossman
books.description: A gifted young knight arrives at Camelot to find it too late—the king died two weeks ago. The surviving knights are the oddballs of the Round Table: the Saracen Knight, Arthur's fool, and Merlin's apprentice who turned on him. Together, they must rebuild.

books.title: The Buffalo Hunter Hunter
books.description: Set in the nascent days of Montana, a Blackfeet Indian named Good Stab haunts the fields looking for justice. A diary found in 2012 reveals a slow massacre and a series of confessional visits from Good Stab, who shares his peculiarly long life.

books.title: The Busybody Book Club
books.description: The five members of the St. Tredock Book Club disagree on everything. When one member suddenly disappears and a dead body is found at his house, these bibliophiles must put their differences aside to solve the mystery.

books.title: The Cemetery of Untold Stories
books.description: When Alma Cruz inherits a small plot of land in the Dominican Republic, she turns it into a graveyard for her unfinished manuscripts and their characters. But the characters have other ideas—and the cemetery becomes a mysterious sanctuary for their true narratives.

books.title: The City and Its Uncertain Walls
books.author: Haruki Murakami

books.title: The Deluge
books.description: In California in 2013, scientist Tony Pietrus receives a death threat. His fate becomes bound to a stunning cast—including a young activist named Kate Morris who begins a project that will alter the course of decades to come.

books.title: The Demon of Unrest
books.author: Erik Larson
books.description: On November 6, 1860, Abraham Lincoln became president. Master storyteller Erik Larson offers a gripping account of the chaotic five months between Lincoln's election and the Confederacy's shelling of Fort Sumter—marked by tragic errors, enflamed egos, and betrayals.

books.title: The Devil Takes You Home
books.description: Buried in debt, Mario reluctantly becomes a hitman. After tragedy destroys the life he knew, he agrees to one final job: hijack a cartel's cash shipment. Along the way, his hidden motivations are laid bare alongside nightmarish encounters that defy explanation.

books.title: The Disordered Cosmos
books.author: Chanda Prescod-Weinstein
books.description: Dr. Chanda Prescod-Weinstein shares her love for physics—the Standard Model, the physics of melanin, dark matter—alongside a perspective informed by history, politics, and Black and queer feminist lineages. A call for a more liberatory practice of science.

books.title: The Ending Writes Itself
books.description: Six struggling authors are invited to spend a weekend on the private Scottish island of bestselling novelist Arthur Fletch. They arrive to discover a shocking secret: Arthur is dead and his last book is unfinished. One of them must write the ending.

books.title: The Expert of Subtle Revisions
books.description: A young woman waits for her father's sailboat. He never shows. The clue: go to the library in Berkeley, find a certain book, follow the instructions. In 1933 Vienna, a young math professor joins an intellectual circle as fascism rises.

books.title: The Eyes Are the Best Part
books.description: Ji-won's family tumbles into disarray after her father's affair. In her dreams, she walks through bloody rooms full of eyes. Salivatingly blue eyes the same shade as George's—her mother's new boyfriend. Ji-won will make sure he doesn't deserve anything.

books.title: The First Time I Saw Him
The Frozen River
books.author: Ariel Lawhon
books.description: Maine, 1789. When a man is entombed in the river ice, midwife Martha Ballard examines the body. Months earlier, she documented an alleged rape by two respected gentlemen—one of whom is now dead. A powerful woman refusing to accept anything less than justice.

books.title: The Genius of Judy
books.description: The Genius of Judy reveals Judy Blume's remarkable story as never before, showing how her books explored the pillars of the growing women's rights movement and why—despite decades of banning—she remains a cultural touchstone today.

books.title: The Ghostwriter
books.author: Freida McFadden
books.description: Abby Hoffman has everything she ever wanted: a perfect marriage, a beautiful home, a career as a ghostwriter. But as she works on her latest project—a memoir for a reclusive bestselling author—she begins to see disturbing parallels between the author's past and her own life.

books.title: The Guest List
books.author: Lucy Foley
books.description: On an island off the coast of Ireland, guests gather for a glamorous wedding. The groom is a rising TV star; the bride a magazine publisher. But as the champagne flows, resentments begin to mingle with the reminiscences. And then someone turns up dead.
books.awards: Ned Kelly Award, CWA Gold Dagger, Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: The Heart Principle
books.author: Helen Hoang
books.description: Violinist Anna Sun accidentally goes viral on YouTube, then burns out trying to replicate the moment. When her boyfriend announces he wants an open relationship, she embarks on a string of one-night stands—including with tattooed motorcycle-rider Quan Diep, who accepts her unconditionally.

books.title: The House Saphir
books.description: Mallory Fontaine is a fraud—she can only see ghosts. When Armand Saphir offers a large sum to rid his ancestral home of his ancestor's ghost, she can't resist. But murder comes to House Saphir and Mallory finds herself at the center.

books.title: The Housemaid
books.author: Freida McFadden
books.description: I clean the Winchesters' beautiful house and collect their daughter from school. I try to ignore how Nina makes a mess just to watch me clean it up. But by the time I realize my attic bedroom door only locks from the outside, it's far too late. The Winchesters don't know who I really am—or what I'm capable of.
books.awards: ITW Thriller Award, Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: The Inheritance Games
books.author: Jennifer Lynn Barnes
books.description: When billionaire Tobias Hawthorne dies, he leaves virtually his entire fortune to Avery Grambs—a teenager who has no idea why. To receive the inheritance, she must move into Hawthorne House, where every room bears the old man's love of puzzles. But the four Hawthorne grandsons who expected to inherit billions are not pleased.
books.awards: Buxtehuder Bulle, Edgar Award, Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: The It Girl
books.author: Ruth Ware
books.description: April was Oxford's ultimate It Girl—until she was murdered by a porter named John Neville. A decade later, a journalist presents new evidence that Neville may have been innocent. Hannah reconnects with old friends, only to realize they all have something to hide—including a murder.
books.awards: Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: The Jasmine Throne
books.author: Tasha Suri
books.description: Imprisoned princess Malini and maidservant Priya—who hides dangerous magic—become unlikely allies. One seeks to depose her dictator brother; the other seeks her lost family. Together, they will change the fate of an empire.

books.title: The Keeper
books.author: Tana French
books.description: On a cold night in remote Ireland, a girl goes missing. Sweet, loving Rachel Holohan was about to be engaged—now she's dead in the river. Retired Chicago detective Cal Hooper has friends in this village, and he owes them loyalty. But uncovering the truth threatens to tear everything apart. The third and final Cal Hooper novel.

books.title: The Last Murder at the End of the World
books.author: Stuart Turton

books.title: The Last Party
books.author: Clare Mackintosh
