books.description: Perla Wultz has kept a decades-old crime as her dark obsession. A doctoral student interviewing the convicted killer is getting him to open up about the truth of what really happened. As past and present entwine, who's deceiving who—and who will be the next to die?

books.title: The Last Thing He Told Me
books.author: Laura Dave
books.description: Before Owen Michaels disappears, he leaves his wife one note: Protect her. Hannah must protect his sixteen-year-old daughter Bailey—who wants nothing to do with her stepmother—while the FBI closes in and Hannah unravels the truth about who Owen really was.
books.awards: Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: The Last Word
books.description: Emma Carpenter lives in isolation house-sitting on the rainy Washington coast. She posts a one-star review of a gruesome horror novel—and then disturbing incidents start occurring at night. Could the author be stalking her?

books.title: The Library of Fates
books.author: Aditi Khorana
books.description: Eleanor has spent her life as an apprentice at the Library of Fates. When her mentor dies and its rarest book—which predicts your future if you confess a past secret—goes missing, she must retrieve it with the mentor's estranged son, her old love.

books.title: The Lion Women of Tehran
books.description: In 1950s Tehran, seven-year-old Ellie bonds with kind Homa at school. Years later, after their lives diverge dramatically, Homa reappears in Ellie's privileged world—and the political turmoil building in Iran brings an earth-shattering betrayal.

books.title: The Loneliness of Sonia and Sunny
The Lost Book of Elizabeth Barton
books.description: Historian Alison Sage discovers a manuscript containing the prophecies of a 16th-century nun executed for condemning Henry VIII. She's invited to an exclusive research manor next to the ruins of the priory—and it becomes the site of a murder.

books.title: The Lost Story of Eva Fuentes
books.description: American expat Margo is hired to source a book that is more than 120 years old and has only one copy in existence. In Havana 1966, librarian Pilar fights Castro's regime through books. In Boston 1900, Cuban teacher Eva attends Harvard and falls into forbidden love.

books.title: The Love Songs of W.E.B. Du Bois
books.author: Honorée Fanonne Jeffers
books.description: Ailey Pearl Garfield bears the names of choreographer Alvin Ailey and her great-grandmother Pearl, descendant of enslaved Georgians. To come to terms with her identity, Ailey embarks on a journey through her family's past—uncovering generations of ancestors in the deep South.

books.title: The Mad Wife
books.description: Lulu Mayfield has spent five years molding herself into the perfect 1950s housewife. When a new neighbor Bitsy moves in, Lulu suspects something darker lurks behind her constant smile. As her fixation deepens, others begin questioning Lulu's sanity.

books.title: The Man Who Died Twice
books.author: Richard Osman
books.description: Elizabeth has received a letter from an old colleague with a big mistake and a very real threat to his life—stolen diamonds, a violent mobster. As bodies start piling up, Elizabeth enlists Joyce, Ibrahim and Ron in the hunt for a ruthless murderer. Can the Thursday Murder Club find the killer before the killer finds them?
books.awards: British Book Award, Bollinger Everyman Wodehouse Prize

books.title: The Merge
books.description: Amelia can't bear to see her mother Laurie's Alzheimer's progress. She signs them up for an experimental merging procedure where her mother's ailing mind will be transferred into Amelia's healthy body. But things at The Village are not as they seem.

books.title: The Message
books.author: Ta-Nehisi Coates
books.description: Ta-Nehisi Coates traveled to Dakar, Columbia SC, and Palestine, reckoning with the gap between narratives we've accepted and clashing realities on the ground—and the urgent need to untangle ourselves from destructive nationalist myths.

books.title: The Ministry for the Future
books.author: Kim Stanley Robinson
books.description: Established in 2025, the Ministry for the Future exists to advocate for the world's future generations. Kim Stanley Robinson's vision of climate change as it will unfold over decades—a world in which we might just overcome the extraordinary challenges we face.

books.title: The Missing Pages
books.description: Harry Widener boards the Titanic holding a priceless book he just purchased. Neither Harry nor the book are seen again. Decades later, Harvard sophomore Violet Hutchins, recovering from her own great loss, works at the Widener Library—and strange things begin happening.

books.title: The Most Wonderful Crime of the Year
books.author: Ally Carter

books.title: The Mysterious Affair at Styles
books.author: Agatha Christie
books.description: Agatha Christie's debut novel introduces Hercule Poirot. When elderly Emily Inglethorp is poisoned at her country estate, Poirot puts his prodigious sleuthing skills to work—navigating a cast of suspects, each with desperate secrets to keep.
books.awards: Audie Award

books.title: The Neighbor Favor
books.description: Shy librarian Lily Greene seeks help getting a date for her sister's wedding from her charming new neighbor Nick Brown—not knowing he's the fantasy author she's been corresponding with for months, who ghosted her.

books.title: The Other Valley
books.author: Scott Alexander Howard
books.description: Sixteen-year-old Odile is vying for a seat on the Conseil that controls her isolated town's borders. On the other side, the same town exists—20 years ahead or behind in time. When she recognizes visitors she wasn't supposed to see, she learns her friend Edme is about to die.

books.title: The Paris Library
books.author: Janet Skeslien Charles
books.description: Paris, 1939. Young Odile Souchet has a dream job at the American Library in Paris. When the Nazis march in, she joins the Resistance with her fellow librarians—using books as their weapons. Montana, 1983: teenager Lily discovers her elderly neighbor's mysterious past.

books.title: The Parisian
books.description: The Parisian illuminates a pivotal period of Palestinian history through the journey and romances of one young man, from his studies in France during World War I to his return to Palestine at the dawn of its battle for independence.
awards: Sue Kaufman Prize for First Fiction

books.title: The Quiet Librarian
books.description: Hana Babic is a quiet librarian who wants to be left alone. When a detective brings news that her best friend has been murdered, she knows the dark remnant of a past she shared with her friend has come for her. She must become the deadly Night Mora again.

books.title: The Reading List
books.author: Sara Nisha Adams
books.description: Widower Mukesh worries about his granddaughter Priya, who hides in her room reading. Aleisha finds a crumpled list of novels in the back of To Kill a Mockingbird at her library job. Shared books create a connection between two lonely souls.

books.title: The Return of Ellie Black
books.author: Emilya Naymark
books.description: When teenager Ellie Black—missing for two years—surfaces alive in the woods, Detective Chelsey Calhoun is assigned the case. But Ellie won't say where she's been or who she's protecting. And if Chelsey can't unravel what happened, the next girl taken might not return.
books.awards: Audie Award, She Reads Best of Award

books.title: The Safekeep
books.author: Yael van der Wouden
books.description: It is 1961 in rural Netherlands. Isabel lives alone in her mother's country home until her brother brings his girlfriend Eva to stay for the season. Isabel develops a fury-fueled obsession—then a discovery that unravels all she has ever known.
awards: The Rooster -- The Morning News Tournament of Books

books.title: The Saint of Bright Doors
books.description: Fetter was raised to kill his sainted father—which gave him plenty to discuss in therapy. After a blood-soaked childhood, he escaped to the big city where divine destinies are a dime a dozen, and every door is laden with potential.

books.title: The School for Good Mothers
books.author: Jessamine Chan
books.description: Frida Liu has a horrible day and leaves her toddler alone briefly. Now the state is measuring her fitness as a mother. In a Big Brother-like institution, she must prove she can live up to standards set for 'good' mothers.
awards: The Rooster -- The Morning News Tournament of Books

books.title: The Sea Captain's Wife
books.description: Nineteen-year-old Mary Ann Patten and her husband set sail in 1856. When Joshua falls dangerously ill and the treacherous first mate agitates for mutiny, Mary Ann steps into the breach—becoming the first woman to command a merchant vessel as captain.

books.title: The Searcher
books.author: Tana French
books.description: Retired detective Cal Hooper moves to a remote village in rural Ireland, planning to leave his old instincts behind. Then a local boy appeals to him: his brother is missing and the police don't seem to care. Something is wrong in this community—and Cal must find out what.
books.awards: Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: The Stardust Grail
books.description: Maya Hoshimoto was once the best art thief in the galaxy, returning stolen artifacts to alien civilizations. Now a grad student haunted by disturbing visions, an old friend brings her one last job: find a powerful object that could save an alien species from extinction.

books.title: The Starseekers
books.description: In the 1960s space race, arcane engineer Cynthia Rhodes investigates a museum theft and a murder at her NASA lab—and discovers a surprising link between both cases. Indiana Jones meets Hidden Figures in a historical fantasy.

books.title: The Strength of the Few
books.description: Vis Telimus won the Iudicium—and lost everything. An ancient device has replicated him across three separate worlds. He has to hide, fight, play politics, train, trust, and lie. Three different bodies, three different lives. He is loved, hated, and entirely alone.

books.title: The Sun and the Starmaker
books.description: In the snowy mountains of the Lost Range, the village of Reverie survives only through the Starmaker's magic. Aurora Finch grows up on tales of the Starmaker—never imagining she'd meet him. On the morning of her wedding, everything changes.

books.title: The Swan's Daughter
books.description: Prince Arris knows that marriage means murder thanks to a poorly worded wish to a sea witch. His only chance at a long life is finding true love. When he meets veritas swan Demelza, she can hear the truth in everyone's words—including his potential brides.

books.title: The Toni Morrison Book Club
books.description: Four friends—Black and white, gay and straight, immigrant and American-born—use Toni Morrison's novels as a springboard for intimate conversations about everyday racism and living whole in times of uncertainty.

books.title: The Violin Conspiracy
books.author: Brendan Slocumb
books.description: Ray McMillian discovers his great-grandfather's fiddle is actually a priceless Stradivarius. With the international Tchaikovsky Competition approaching, it's stolen. Now his family and the descendants of the man who once enslaved Ray's ancestor both claim it belongs to them.

books.title: The Wager
books.author: David Grann
books.description: On January 28, 1742, thirty emaciated men wash up on the coast of Brazil. They are survivors of His Majesty's Ship the Wager. But six months later, three castaways in Chile tell a very different story. The Admiralty convenes a court martial.

books.title: The Wayfinder
books.description: An epic novel set in the Polynesian islands during the height of the Tu'i Tonga Empire. Kōrero, a young girl chosen to save her people from starvation, embarks on a daring seafaring journey across a vast ocean empire.
awards: The Rooster -- The Morning News Tournament of Books

books.title: The Whalebone Theatre
books.description: Cristabel Seagrave is an unwanted orphan who grows into an unmarriageable young woman in a traditional English manor by the sea. She is determined to write her own story. As war approaches, the three Seagrave children find the roles they were assigned are no longer ones they want.

books.title: The Wide Wide Sea
books.author: Hampton Sides
books.description: Captain James Cook, already lionized as the greatest explorer in British history, set off on his third voyage in 1776. Two-and-a-half years later, on a beach in Hawaii, he was killed. Hampton Sides' bravura account asks: how did it come to this?

books.title: The Wild Card
books.description: Jordan Hathaway is more than comfortable behind her bar at the team's favorite watering hole. When her father threatens to sell the Vancouver Storm hockey team, she's forced to work with the one man who likes everyone but her: Coach Tate Ward.

books.title: The Write Off
books.description: Thirteen years ago, Mars Darling met West Emerson outside their writing class. They became best friends, then briefly lovers—until he betrayed her. Now he's at the same book festival and Mars's assumptions begin to shift.

books.title: The Zorg
books.description: In late October 1780, a slave ship set sail. After being captured by a privateer, the renamed ship was crammed with 442 enslaved people. Running out of water, the crew proposed throwing dozens overboard. The legal case that followed sparked the abolitionist movement.

books.title: There Are Rivers in the Sky
books.description: In 1840 London, Arthur's gift for memory earns him a spot at a leading publisher. In 2014 Turkey, ten-year-old Yazidi girl Narin races to be baptized before going deaf. In 2018 London, hydrologist Zaleekah decides to take her own life—until a curious book changes everything.

books.title: This Book Made Me Think of You
This Book Won't Burn
books.description: After being uprooted to a new school for her senior year, Noor Khan discovers hundreds of books have been labeled 'obscene' and removed from the library—virtually all by queer and BIPOC authors. She can't sit back and do nothing.

books.title: This House Will Feed
books.description: In Ireland's Great Famine of 1848, Maggie O'Shaughnessy is offered escape from the workhouse by mysterious Lady Catherine—if she will impersonate her late daughter to hoodwink solicitors. But something in the house is reawakening buried memories.

books.title: This Story Might Save Your Life
books.description: Benny Abbott and Joy Moore host one of the most beloved podcasts in the world. Each week, they delight listeners with a different 'against all odds' survival story. When Benny arrives to record and finds shattered glass and an empty house, the one clue is an incomplete memoir. Now Benny must find them before it's too late—and before the police zero in on him as their prime suspect.

books.title: Tourist Season
books.description: Harper Starling protects her seaside town of Cape Carnage—and her award-winning flowerbeds hide troublesome tourists. Then Nolan Rhodes arrives, handsome and charming, on an anniversary revenge mission. Harper isn't the monster he expected.

books.title: Trial of the Sun Queen
books.description: Lor has endured twelve years of torment under the Aurora King. When she's thrust into the Sun Queen Trials, competing against nine other Tributes for the role of queen, she realizes she must win—because if she loses, she dies.

books.title: Tusk Love
books.description: Guinevere longs for adventure, not the life of a nobleman's wife. When her caravan is attacked by bandits, her only chance of survival is to travel with Oskar, an aloof half-orc—and despite his cold exterior, sparks begin to fly.

books.title: Unabridged
books.description: Journalist Stefan Fatsis embedded as a lexicographer-in-training at Merriam-Webster, learning how words get into the dictionary, where they come from, who decides what they mean—and how they are weaponized in today's polarized culture.

books.title: Unread: A Memoir
books.description: Oliver James graduated from high school as one of over eight million functionally illiterate Americans. At age 34, he dedicated himself to learning to read—and became a BookTok sensation documenting his journey. In 365 days he went from barely reading a menu to closing in on 100 books.

books.title: Vagabond
books.description: Tim Curry takes readers behind the scenes of his rise from military brat to Tony-nominated actor, covering The Rocky Horror Picture Show, It, Clue, Spamalot, and his pivot to voicework after a catastrophic stroke in 2012.

books.title: Vera Wong's Unsolicited Advice for Murderers
books.author: Jesse Q. Sutanto

books.title: Vigil
books.description: Jill 'Doll' Blaine has performed her sacred duty of ushering souls into the afterlife 343 times. Her newest charge, oil company CEO K.J. Boone, will not be consoled—because he has nothing to regret. A Saunders novel taking on corporate greed and capitalism.

books.title: Violet Thistlewaite Is Not a Villain Anymore
We Are All Guilty Here
books.author: C.J. Cooke
books.description: When two teenage girls vanish the night of the fireworks in North Falls, Officer Emmy Clifton's investigation reveals dark secrets nobody knew about the girls. Every teenage girl has secrets—but who would kill for them?
books.awards: Goodreads Choice Award, She Reads Best of Award

books.title: We Love You, Bunny
books.description: Sam has just published her first novel to critical acclaim. But at a book tour stop, her old frenemies—the Bunnies—furious at how they've been portrayed, kidnap her. Now it's their turn to tell their side of the story.

books.title: We Survived the Night
books.description: Born to a Secwepemc father and Jewish-Irish mother, Julian Brave Noisecat's childhood was full of contradictions. He sets across the country to correct the erasure of Native Americans while developing his voice as a storyteller and reckoning with his relationship with his father.

books.title: We the People
books.description: Harvard professor Jill Lepore argues that the framers never intended the Constitution to be kept under glass—they expected future generations would forever be tinkering with it. A sweeping, illustrated history of every attempt to amend America.

books.title: We Used to Live Here
books.description: As a young, queer couple who flip houses, Charlie and Eve can't believe the killer deal on their new home. Then a knock on the door—a man claiming to have lived there years before wants to show his kids around. Eve lets them in, and inexplicable things begin.

books.title: Wellness
books.description: When Jack and Elizabeth meet as college students in the '90s, they quickly join forces in Chicago's underground art scene. Twenty years and a marriage later, they encounter cults disguised as mindfulness groups, polyamorous would-be suitors, and Facebook wars—and struggle to recognize each other.

books.title: What Stalks the Deep
books.description: Alex Easton does not want to visit America—especially not an abandoned coal mine in West Virginia with a reputation for being haunted. But when their old friend summons them to help find his lost cousin who went missing in that very mine, a sworn soldier has no choice.

books.title: What You Are Looking for Is in the Library
books.author: Michiko Aoyama

books.title: When the Sea Came Alive
books.author: Garrett M. Graff
books.description: From Pulitzer finalist Garrett Graff, the most up-to-date and complete account of D-Day—from the secret creation of landing plans to the moment 150,000 Allied troops hit the beaches, and the human stories of those five defining weeks of WWII.

books.title: When the Tides Held the Moon
books.description: Blacksmith Benny Caldera creates an ironwork tank for Coney Island's Luna Park in 1910s New York. The tank will hold a live merman stolen from the East River—and what begins as a friendship of necessity between Benny and the merman Río deepens into love.

books.title: Where the Wildflowers Grow
books.description: Leigh is the last of the Wildes—she watched them all die. When the prison transport bus careens off the road, she survives again, stumbling upon an unexpected sanctuary: a flower farm in rural Alabama with a found family of its own.

books.title: Whidbey
books.author: Elizabeth George
books.description: Birdie Chang is on the run when she arrives on Whidbey Island, desperate to escape Calvin Boyer—the man who abused her as a child and has now resurfaced. A fellow survivor wrote a bestselling memoir about Calvin's crimes. When Calvin turns up murdered, both women begin a desperate search for answers in this complex whodunnit about justice and who gets to tell the story.

books.title: Why We Read
books.author: Shannon Reed
books.description: Shannon Reed makes the case that we should read for pleasure above all else. In this whip-smart, funny collection, she shares surprising stories from her life as a reader and the poignant ways books have impacted her students.

books.title: Wild Rain
books.description: A reporter has come to Wyoming to do a story on doctors for his Black newspaper. He thinks the doctor will be interesting until he meets the doctor's sister, Spring—who runs her own ranch and wears denim pants instead of dresses.

books.title: Wild Reverence
books.description: Matilda is the youngest goddess of her clan, blessed with messenger magic. When mortal Vincent wrote to her on the darkest night of his life—a letter she never answered—their fated reunion across the realms sets in motion a story of magic, gods, and finally allowing herself to be loved.

books.title: Woman Down
books.description: Her words used to set the page on fire. But a viral backlash forced Petra Rose to take a hiatus. She retreats to a secluded lakeside cabin, where Detective Nathaniel Saint arrives with disturbing news—and ignites a creativity she thought long since burned out.

books.title: Women of a Promiscuous Nature
books.description: Ruth Foster is stopped by the local sheriff on a walk through her North Carolina hometown and taken to a health clinic—despite never having shared more than a chaste kiss with a man. She is one of dozens of women held at the State Industrial Farm Colony.

books.title: Writing Creativity and Soul
books.description: Sue Monk Kidd's pilgrimage into the mysteries and methods of the writerly life—equal parts memoir, guidebook, and spiritual quest. At its heart: the unwavering belief that writing is a spiritual act drawing inspiration from the soul.

books.title: Writing During the Apocalypse
books.description: Rising authoritarianism. Covid. Inflation. War. Climate change. In captivating prose, Ed Simon grapples with what it's like to write during what could be the last decades of human civilization—arguing that literary expression can endow a meaningless world with meaning.

books.title: Yellowface
books.description: When June witnesses Athena Liu's death in a freak accident, she acts on impulse—stealing Athena's just-finished manuscript about Chinese laborers in World War I and submitting it as her own work.

books.title: You Are Fatally Invited
books.description: When renowned anonymous author J.R. Alastor hires former writer Mila del Angél to host a retreat at his private Maine island, she jumps at the chance—partly to plot a murder of her own. But when a guest turns up dead, it's not the murder she planned.

books.title: You Did Nothing Wrong
books.description: Single mother Elodie's life has become a fairy tale—new husband, crumbling house being renovated, baby on the way. Then her autistic son Jude claims he can hear voices in the walls. One secret Elodie has been hiding is about to be revealed.

books.title: You Weren't Meant to Be Human
books.description: A chilling debut adult novel combining alien invasion with folk horror set in the remote Scottish Highlands.

books.title: Glorious Exploits
books.author: Ferdia Lennon
books.description: On the island of Sicily amid the Peloponnesian War, two unemployed potters hatch a plan to stage Euripides' Medea for Athenian prisoners of war. An unforgettable ode to the power of art in a time of war and brotherhood in a time of enmity.
books.awards: British Book Award, Bollinger Everyman Wodehouse Prize, Dylan Thomas Prize, Walter Scott Prize

books.author: Jennifer Marie Thorne
books.awards: Goodreads Choice Award, RUSA CODES Reading List

books.title: The Luminaries
books.author: Eleanor Catton
books.description: 1866: Young Walter Moody stumbles across a secret gathering of twelve men investigating a series of unexplained events in the New Zealand goldfields. A brilliantly constructed mystery as complex and ornate as the night sky.
books.awards: Booker Prize, Dylan Thomas Prize, Women's Prize for Fiction, Walter Scott Prize, Goodreads Choice Award

books.author: Bothayna Al-Essa
books.awards: National Book Award Finalist, ALTA National Translation Award

books.author: Alexis Wright
books.awards: Miles Franklin Literary Award, The Stella Prize, Dublin Literary Award

books.author: Hwang Bo-Reum
books.awards: Goodreads Choice Award

books.author: Paul Murray
books.awards: Booker Prize, Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: Workhorse
books.author: Caroline Palmer
books.description: At the turn of the millennium, editorial assistant Clodagh Harmon claws for status at a prestigious fashion magazine despite lacking the right pedigree. A richly drawn, wickedly funny story of envy, ambition, and female desire in New York's media world.

books.author: Charlotte McConaghy
books.awards: Audie Award, Edgar Award, Women's Prize for Fiction, Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: One Day, Everyone Will Have Always Been Against This
books.author: Omar El Akkad
books.description: Award-winning journalist Omar El Akkad's reckoning with what it means to live in a West that has broken its promise—and a heartsick breakup letter with the ideals that drew him here as an immigrant.
books.awards: National Book Award, National Book Critics Circle Award, Goodreads Choice Award

books.author: Antonia Hodgson
books.awards: Goodreads Choice Award, Waterstones Book of the Year

books.author: Nnedi Okorafor
books.awards: Nebula Award, Los Angeles Times Book Prize, Goodreads Choice Award

books.author: Antonia Hodgson
books.awards: Goodreads Choice Award, Waterstones Book of the Year

books.title: And Now, Back to You
books.author: B.K. Borison
books.description: Radio meteorologist Jackson Clark and field reporter Delilah Stewart clash constantly—until they're forced to partner for the snowstorm of the century. An opposites-attract romance with When Harry Met Sally energy.

books.title: The CIA Book Club
books.author: Charlie English
books.description: The astonishing story of the ten million books—from Orwell to Agatha Christie—that US intelligence smuggled across the Iron Curtain to undermine Soviet censorship and bring ideas to real readers in the East.

books.title: Saltcrop
books.author: Yume Kitasei
books.description: In a near-future drowned world, sailor Skipper and her sister cross a dying ocean to find their missing third sibling—who may hold the key to Earth's environmental salvation.

books.title: The Tragedy of True Crime
books.author: John J. Lennon
books.description: In 2001, John J. Lennon killed a man on a Brooklyn street. Writing from behind bars as a prison journalist, he tells the full life stories of four men serving time for the lives they took—a first-of-its-kind work of immersive prison journalism.

books.title: Ladies in Hating
books.author: Alexandra Vasti
books.description: Celebrated Regency authoress Lady Georgiana sets out to unmask her literary rival—only to discover she's actually Cat Lacey, her long-secret infatuation. A swoony, steamy sapphic rivals-to-lovers romance.
books.awards: Lambda Literary Award

books.title: The Secret Book Society
books.author: Madeline Martin
books.description: London, 1895: three women trapped by oppressive marriages receive a mysterious invitation to a secret book club—a Victorian sanctuary where they find freedom, sisterhood, and the courage to rewrite their own stories.
books.awards: Goodreads Choice Award

books.author: Kiran Desai
books.awards: Booker Prize, Kirkus Prize

books.title: The Correspondent
books.author: Virginia Evans
books.description: At seventy-three, Sybil Van Antwerp has used her witty, wise letters to make sense of the world—while keeping loved ones at arm's length. Letters from her past now force her to examine what she has been avoiding for thirty years.
books.awards: Audie Award, Women's Prize for Fiction, Goodreads Choice Award, The Center for Fiction First Novel Prize

books.author: qntm
books.description: A novel-length exploration of infinite regress, self-referential fiction, and the nature of consciousness—a mind-bending puzzle-box story for fans of complex narrative structures.

books.title: Sisters in the Wind
books.author: Angeline Boulley
books.description: Foster teen Lucy Smith has been running since her father died five years ago. When strangers reveal her Ojibwe heritage and the family she never knew, she must decide who to trust—while someone from her past closes in.
books.awards: Michael L. Printz Award, American Indian Youth Literature Award

books.author: Alix E. Harrow
books.awards: Audie Award

books.title: Snake-Eater
books.author: T. Kingfisher
books.description: Selena flees her past to claim her late aunt's desert house—only to discover the land is haunted by an ancient god called Snake-Eater, who has developed an unsettling obsession with the property's new owner.

books.title: Mate
books.author: Ali Hazelwood
books.description: Serena Paris is the first Human-Were hybrid—and a target. With enemies closing in, she turns to the Alpha whose pack authority stops at nothing to protect his mate, even if Serena doesn't return his feelings.
books.awards: Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: Local Heavens
books.author: K.M. Fajardo
books.description: New York City, 2075: corporate hacker Nick Carraway investigates the alluring new-money Jay Gatsby in a fractured, inequality-ravaged future. A brilliant cyberpunk reimagining of The Great Gatsby.

books.title: Murder Your Darlings
books.author: Jenna Blum
books.description: Blocked novelist Sam Vetiver connects with stratospherically successful author William Corwyn—but as writers in his orbit turn up dead, she must ask whether her charismatic new partner is everything he seems.

books.author: Megan Shepherd
books.awards: Manly Wade Wellman Award

books.title: I'm Glad My Mom Died
books.author: Jennette McCurdy
books.description: A heartbreaking and hilarious memoir by iCarly star Jennette McCurdy about eating disorders, addiction, and a complicated relationship with her overbearing mother—and how she finally retook control of her life.
books.awards: ALA Alex Award, Lincoln Award, Goodreads Choice Award

books.author: Julia Armfield
books.awards: Lambda Literary Award, The Kitschies, The Polari Prize, Goodreads Choice Award

books.title: Disorientation
books.author: Elaine Hsieh Chou
books.description: PhD student Ingrid Yang accidentally uncovers a bombshell in the archives that upends her sheltered academic life and ignites a campus firestorm. A blistering satire of privilege and power for fans of Paul Beatty and Charles Yu.
books.awards: New York Public Library Young Lions Fiction Award, Thurber Prize, CALA Best Book Award

books.author: Shauna Robinson
books.awards: HOLT Medallion by Virginia Romance Writers

books.title: The Song of the Cell
books.author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
books.description: The Pulitzer Prize-winning author of The Emperor of All Maladies traces the story of cells—from their seventeenth-century discovery to the cutting-edge therapies now using them to create new humans. A masterpiece on what it means to be human.
books.awards: PROSE Award, Chautauqua Prize, Goodreads Choice Award

books.author: Jesse Q. Sutanto
books.awards: Barry Award, Audie Award, Edgar Award, Goodreads Choice Award

books.author: Janice Hallett
books.awards: British Book Award

books.author: Barbara Davis
books.awards: Goodreads Choice Award

books.author: Tania James
books.awards: National Book Award, Libby Award, Carol Shields Prize

books.title: The Glowing Hours
books.author: Leila Siddiqui
books.description: Summer 1816: Indian housemaid Mehrunissa Begum accompanies the Shelleys to Lake Geneva, where Mary begins Frankenstein—and the villa descends into madness. A gothic horror story told from the margins of literary history.

books.title: The Irish Goodbye
books.author: Beth Ann Fennelly
books.description: Micro-memoirs exploring the often-overlooked moments that shape a life—from a brush with an old flame to the devastating death of a sister. A genre-defying collection about family, marriage, and motherhood.

books.title: The Sisters of Book Row
books.author: Shelley Noble
books.description: 1915: three Applebaum sisters run a rare bookshop on Manhattan's Book Row—while Anthony Comstock, America's most vicious censor, threatens anyone who distributes literature he deems obscene. A timely historical novel about books and the women who refuse to be silenced.

books.title: Monsters in the Archives
books.author: Caroline Bicks
books.description: The first scholar granted access to Stephen King's private archives uncovers manuscripts, hand-written revisions, and alternative endings—and asks what makes King's horror stick in our heads decades after we've closed the book.

books.title: Kin
books.author: Tayari Jones
books.description: Vernice and Annie, two motherless daughters raised in Louisiana, grow up to live starkly different lives—one rising through Spelman College's sisterhood, the other crossing a world of peril and love to find the mother who abandoned her.

books.title: Unfinished Business
books.author: Vivian Gornick
books.description: Nine essays returning to Lawrence, Colette, Marguerite Duras, and others at crucial points in a long life—a celebration of passionate reading by one of America's most celebrated literary critics.
books.awards: PEN/Diamonstein-Spielvogel Award for the Art of the Essay, National Book Critics Circle Award

books.title: Trust
books.author: Hernan Diaz
books.description: Four competing narratives circle a legendary Wall Street tycoon and his enigmatic wife in 1920s New York—each revealing new layers of privilege, deception, and the reality-warping force of capital. A brilliant literary puzzle.
books.awards: Booker Prize, Pulitzer Prize, Kirkus Prize, Goodreads Choice Award

books.author: Simon Jimenez
books.awards: British Fantasy Award, Ignyte Award, Ursula K. Le Guin Prize

books.title: The Grace of Kings
books.author: Ken Liu
books.description: Wily, charming Kuni Garu, a bandit, and stern, fearless Mata Zyndu, the son of a deposed duke, seem like polar opposites. Yet, in the uprising against the emperor, the two quickly become the best of friends after a series of adventures fighting against vast conscripted armies, silk-draped airships, and shapeshifting gods. Once the emperor has been overthrown, however, they each find themselves the leader of separate factions—two sides with very different ideas about how the world should be run and the meaning of justice.

books.title: Middlesex
books.author: Jeffrey Eugenides
books.description: A dazzling triumph—the astonishing tale of a gene that passes down through three generations of a Greek-American family and flowers in the body of a teenage girl.

books.title: These Precious Days: Essays
books.author: Ann Patchett
books.description: The beloved New York Times bestselling author reflects on home, family, friendships and writing in this deeply personal collection of essays.
awards: PEN/Diamonstein-Spielvogel Award for the Art of the Essay
